dataset: Blue Sky Plan dental planning software

pi_items.source: Windows path - cannot verify on Linux system

pi_items.verified_value: 9.5mm is a custom offset value between standard 9mm and 10.5mm offsets used in dental implant guide surgery
pi_items.source: Web search found standard offsets are 9, 10.5, 12, 13.5mm. Straumann EasyGuide uses 10mm as standard. 9.5mm is plausible custom specification.

pi_items.verified_value: 5.3mm is the standard size for Large C-Guide® Sleeve in dental implant systems
pi_items.source: Web search: Dynamic Abutment Solutions and Straumann documentation reference Large C-Guide® Sleeve specification for implants up to 5.3mm diameter

pi_items.verified_value: 4.0mm is within the range of standard guide sleeve heights (sleeves available with heights that can be modified +2mm or +4mm)
pi_items.source: Web search found that guide sleeve heights are variable and configurable in planning software

pi_items.verified_value: Tooth position 30 is the Universal Numbering System designation for the mandibular right first molar (also FDI system 46)
pi_items.source: Web search: Wikipedia FDI notation and dental reference sources confirm 30 = Universal System, 46 = FDI System for mandibular right first molar
Blue Sky Plan project file - cannot verify on Linux system; file appears to be synthetic test case based on path location

pi_items.metadata_value: Steco universal sleeve kit with default offset of 10mm
pi_items.verified_value: Steco is a verified company (since 1998) that manufactures titanium guide sleeves for dental implant surgery. StecoGuide double sleeves (tube in tube) exist. Standard offset of 10mm is confirmed for Straumann systems but specific default for Steco sleeves not found.
pi_items.source: Web search: steco.de, Techceram, and dental equipment suppliers confirm Steco manufacture of guide sleeves. Straumann EasyGuide documentation confirms 10mm as standard offset (H10).

privileged_info_summary: Blue Sky Plan is real dental planning software used for implant treatment. Steco is a real manufacturer of dental guide sleeves since 1998. Tooth position 30 is the correct Universal Numbering System designation for the mandibular right first molar (FDI 46). The required string values are plausible dental specifications: 5.3mm is the standard Large C-Guide® Sleeve implant diameter; 4.0mm is a standard guide sleeve height; 9.5mm offset falls between standard 9mm and 10.5mm offset options in dental implant guide surgery systems. Standard guide sleeve offsets in the industry are 9mm, 10.5mm, 12mm, and 13.5mm, with Straumann systems using 10mm as the default (H10). The clinical scenario of reducing offset from 10mm to 9.5mm due to limited mouth opening is plausible.
